title: Festuca loricata
intro: Festuca loricata, commonly known as broom fescue or festuca, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Festuca loricata contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, with some of the most common preparations including teas for relaxation and sleep promotion.
taxonomy: Festuca loricata is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. This species belongs to the genus Festuca, a group of grasses commonly known as fescues. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants. The classification of Festuca loricata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Its classification also serves as a tool for understanding its relationships with other plant species.
